title: QM Master Data
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of QM Master Data.
Identify the master data objects used in SAP QM.
Understand how master data supports quality inspections.
Learn commonly used SAP QM transaction codes.
Understand integration of QM Master Data with other SAP modules.

introduction.title: What is QM Master Data?
introduction.content: QM Master Data consists of all the information required to perform quality inspections and quality control activities in SAP. It defines inspection methods, inspection characteristics, sampling procedures, inspection plans and quality specifications that are reused during production and procurement.

business_purpose.title: Why is QM Master Data Important?
business_purpose.content: Well-maintained master data ensures consistent inspections, standardized quality processes and accurate inspection results. It allows SAP to automatically determine inspection requirements whenever an inspection lot is created.

master_data_objects.name: Material Master
master_data_objects.purpose: Stores quality inspection settings for each material.

master_data_objects.name: Inspection Type
master_data_objects.purpose: Defines when and why inspections are performed.

master_data_objects.name: Sampling Procedure
master_data_objects.purpose: Defines sample size and inspection methodology.

master_data_objects.name: Catalogs and Code Groups
master_data_objects.purpose: Standardizes defect recording and usage decisions.

inspection_types.description: Goods Receipt Inspection for Purchase Orders.

sampling_information.title: Sampling Procedures
sampling_information.content: Sampling procedures determine how many units or samples are inspected from a batch. Instead of inspecting every item, SAP can calculate statistically valid sample sizes based on predefined rules.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A stainless steel coil requires testing for thickness, width, surface finish and hardness. These inspection characteristics are created once as Master Inspection Characteristics and reused across multiple Inspection Plans, ensuring consistent quality inspections.

best_practices: Keep master data synchronized with engineering specifications.
important_notes: Inspection characteristics should be standardized across the organization.
Master data should be reviewed whenever product specifications change.
Accurate master data improves inspection consistency and reporting.
